details: The “Award of ₹20,000/- to Best Writers” scheme by the Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu, aimed at recognizing and promoting literary talent within the Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Christian-converted communities. A total of 10 writers from these communities will be selected to receive a cash prize. Additionally, one writer from any other community will also be chosen.
benefits: ₹20,000/- to each of the selected writers (10 from Adi Dravidar/Tribal communities and one from other communities).
Selected writers receive state recognition for their literary contributions.
eligibility: The beneficiary should be a resident of Tamil Nadu.
The beneficiary should be an unemployed writer.
The beneficiary should belong to the Adi Dravidar/ Tribal/ Adi Dravidar converted to Christian community.

offline: The scheme will be advertised in newspapers during April/May.
Interested candidates must submit their literary works along with the required documents to the designated authority as mentioned in the advertisement.

documents_required: Proof of identity (Aadhar card or any other document as directed by the authority).
A copy of the submitted literary work.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the “Award of ₹20,000/- to Best Writers” scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides a cash award of ₹20,000/- to the top 10 writers from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, or Adi Dravidar communities converted to Christianity, with one additional spot for a writer from other communities.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this award?
faqs.answer: Writers from the Adi Dravidar, Tribal, or Adi Dravidar Christian-converted communities are eligible to apply. Additionally, one writer from any other community will be selected.

faqs.question: What is the award amount?
faqs.answer: The award amount is ₹20,000/- for each selected writer.

faqs.question: How many writers will be selected?
faqs.answer: A total of 10 writers from the specified communities and one writer from another community will be selected.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for applicants?
faqs.answer: There is no specified age limit for this scheme.

faqs.question: Is there an income criterion for eligibility?
faqs.answer: There is no income limit required for this scheme.

faqs.question: When will the scheme be announced?
faqs.answer: The scheme will be announced every year in leading newspapers during April and May.

faqs.question: What types of literary works are accepted?
faqs.answer: Original literary works in any genre are accepted for submission.

faqs.question: How should the literary works be submitted?
faqs.answer: Applicants must follow the submission guidelines provided in the newspaper announcements.

faqs.question: Can writers from other communities apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, one spot is reserved for a writer from a community other than Adi Dravidar or Tribal.

faqs.question: What documents are required for submission?
faqs.answer: Applicants are required to submit proof of community, proof of residence, proof of identity, and the literary work.

faqs.question: Is there an application fee?
faqs.answer: No, there is no application fee required to participate.

faqs.question: Where can more information about the scheme be found?
faqs.answer: Detailed information will be published in leading newspapers and can also be obtained from the designated state welfare offices during the scheme’s announcement period.
